questions.question: Explain the difference between error detection and error correction in computer networks. List three common error detection techniques.
keyPoints: Error detection identifies if data was corrupted during transmission
Error correction not only detects but also identifies and fixes errors
Common techniques: Parity check, Checksum, CRC
Error correction methods include Hamming code and ARQ protocols

questions.question: With the help of a suitable example, explain how a single parity bit method works for error detection. Differentiate between even parity and odd parity.
keyPoints: Parity bit added to make total number of 1s even (even parity) or odd (odd parity)
Example: Data 1011001 has 4 ones, even parity bit = 0, odd parity bit = 1
Simple method but can only detect single-bit errors
Cannot detect even number of bit errors

questions.question: Explain the Hamming code error correction method. Encode the data word 1101 using Hamming code with even parity. Show how a single-bit error in position 5 can be detected and corrected.
keyPoints: Hamming code uses redundant parity bits at specific positions (1,2,4,8...)
Data bits are placed in non-parity positions
Each parity bit checks specific bit positions using binary representation
Error position is determined by combining incorrect parity bits (syndrome)
For 1101: codeword = 1110101 (with parity bits at positions 1,2,4)

questions.question: (a) Explain the Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) method for error detection with a suitable example. (b) Explain the three types of ARQ protocols: Stop-and-Wait ARQ, Go-Back-N ARQ, and Selective Repeat ARQ, highlighting their differences.
keyPoints: CRC uses polynomial division - divisor is generator polynomial, remainder is CRC
Example: Data 11010, divisor 1011, CRC remainder appended
CRC can detect burst errors and is widely used in data link layers
Stop-and-Wait: sender waits for ACK before next frame, simple but inefficient
Go-Back-N: retransmits all frames from error point, efficient for low error rates
Selective Repeat: retransmits only errored frame, complex but most efficient
